codeReview.summary: The feature correctly performs pre-LLM Muninn auto-recall in Muninn mode, injects a bounded non-persistent `Relevant Memory (Muninn Auto-Recall)` block into dynamic context, and leaves persisted session history clean. The reviewed commit only retargets validation plumbing to the approved `default` vault and is consistent with the already-landed recall implementation and tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to bypass the literal Windows gateway and claweb_frontdoor start/stop commands in .factory/services.yaml because Exec-mode interpolation stripped PowerShell $env: and $_ expressions, so the documented commands are not reliably executable as written in this environment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature handoff 2026-03-19T05-19-27-195Z__muninn-auto-recall-planner-and-injection__902a0234-60b7-4fb6-aa80-c7f456d3b608.json records skillFeedback.deviations for steps 1.7 Start Services and 3.1 Environment Cleanup, and recommends an Exec-mode-safe Windows variant.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission knowledge still documents the local Muninn MCP listener as unhealthy, but this reviewed feature only depends on REST-based auto-recall and succeeded after switching validation to the approved `default` vault. That split is important shared context for later validators reviewing coexistence assertions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: D:\tmp\claw\picoclaw\.factory\library\muninn-transparent-layer.md says `current PicoClaw MCP negotiation against the live Muninn listener is not yet healthy`, while the worker handoff reports successful live auto-recall via REST on vault `default` and separately notes the MCP listener issue as non-blocking for this feature.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the mission artifacts, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, commit 22e3e0b336bfa405133f1ef97323a6049322c20a, and the relevant agent/context code paths. The implementation satisfies the feature scope for recall-first injection and non-persistent dynamic context, and the main follow-ups are shared-state notes for Windows-safe service commands and the separate MCP-health context.
